title: Creating the NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra NVFP4 Checkpoint with NVIDIA Model Optimizer | NVIDIA Technical Blog
gist: NVIDIA quantized its 550B Nemotron 3 Ultra model to NVFP4 using a novel 'four-over-six' adaptive block scaling method, achieving up to 5.9x higher inference throughput versus GLM-5.1 754B FP4 while matching BF16 accuracy on nearly every benchmark. The model shrinks from 1,121 GB to 352.3 GB (3.2x compression) and runs on both Hopper and Blackwell architectures from a single checkpoint, with quantization time reduced from 85 to 9 minutes via distributed Megatron-LM parallelism.

themes.theme: NVFP4 Quantization Performance and Throughput Gains

insights.claim: NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 Ultra NVFP4 achieves up to 5.9x higher inference throughput than GLM-5.1 754B FP4 on decode-heavy workloads while matching BF16 accuracy across nearly every benchmark.
insights.speaker: Seonghee Lee
insights.so_what: NVFP4 quantization on Blackwell delivers step-change throughput gains over competing FP4 models, strengthening NVIDIA's inference hardware moat.
insights.quote: a model that achieves up to 5.9x higher inference throughput than GLM-5.1 754B FP4 model on decode-heavy workloads while matching BF16 accuracy across nearly every benchmark

insights.claim: NVFP4 quantization compresses Nemotron 3 Ultra from 1,121 GB (BF16) to 352.3 GB, a 3.2x size reduction that cuts hardware footprint in half.
insights.speaker: Seonghee Lee
insights.so_what: 3.2x model compression directly reduces GPU memory requirements, enabling deployment on fewer or smaller GPUs and lowering inference cost per token.
insights.quote: After NVFP4 quantization, the Nemotron 3 Ultra model shrinks from 1,121 GB in BF16 down to 352.3 GB, a 3.2x reduction. The payoff is substantial, cutting the hardware footprint in half.
insights.locator: The Nemotron 3 Ultra NVFP4 checkpoint section

insights.claim: NVFP4's minimum effective bits-per-element is 4.5 due to per-block and per-tensor scaling overhead, meaning pure FP4 (4-bit) storage is not achievable in practice.
insights.speaker: Seonghee Lee
insights.so_what: Investors and engineers should understand that advertised 4-bit models carry overhead; effective bit budgets above 4 bits constrain how far compression can go.
insights.quote: NVFP4 adds per-block and per-tensor scaling overhead, bringing its minimum to 4.5 effective bits-per-element.

themes.theme: Four-Over-Six Adaptive Block Scaling: Novel Quantization Technique

insights.claim: NVIDIA's 'four-over-six' scaling method for NVFP4 reduces median weight reconstruction MSE by 16.4% versus standard max calibration across 49,152 projection weights in Nemotron 3 Ultra's MoE layers.
insights.speaker: Seonghee Lee
insights.so_what: A proprietary calibration technique gives NVIDIA's quantization pipeline a quality edge over standard approaches, potentially creating a moat in enterprise model deployment.
insights.quote: it cut the median reconstruction MSE by 16.4% compared to standard max calibration, and delivered the best downstream result in the balanced 5.03-BPE setting: 98.5% median recovery relative to BF16

insights.claim: Four-over-six scaling independently selects between M=4 and M=6 grid maximums per weight block, avoiding the 13%+ rounding error that occurs when NVFP4's grid gap between 4 and 6 forces rounding in that range.
insights.speaker: Seonghee Lee
insights.so_what: Understanding the FP4 grid gap explains why naive quantization degrades accuracy and why per-block adaptive scaling is necessary for production-quality models.
insights.quote: Any weight that falls in that range gets rounded aggressively to either 4 or 6, sometimes incurring over 13% error on a single value.

insights.claim: NVFP4_FOUR_OVER_SIX_CFG support is scheduled to land in NVIDIA Model Optimizer version 0.46 in July 2026, making this technique publicly available to all developers.
insights.speaker: Seonghee Lee
insights.so_what: Democratizing four-over-six scaling will accelerate FP4 model deployment by third parties, expanding the ecosystem and demand for Blackwell hardware.
insights.quote: NVFP4_FOUR_OVER_SIX_CFG will be released on the upcoming 0.46 NVIDIA Model Optimizer in July.

insights.claim: Four-over-six scaling raises the global per-tensor weight scale by 1.75x when applied to Nemotron 3 Ultra's MoE routed expert layers, reflecting the systematic upward bias of choosing between M=4 and M=6 grids.
insights.speaker: Seonghee Lee
insights.so_what: Quantization practitioners can calibrate expected scale shifts when applying four-over-six to similar MoE architectures.
insights.quote: Four-over-six was used to set the FP4 routed-expert weight scales in Nemotron 3 Ultra, raising the global per-tensor weight scale by 1.75x

themes.theme: Mixed-Precision Checkpoint Strategy Across Layer Types

insights.claim: Nemotron 3 Ultra's NVFP4 checkpoint is not uniformly FP4: MoE routed experts use NVFP4, shared experts and Mamba mixers use FP8 per-tensor, attention linears and embedding layers remain in BF16, and KV cache uses FP8.
insights.speaker: Seonghee Lee
insights.so_what: Real-world quantization requires per-layer precision tuning; blanket FP4 claims are misleading and layer-sensitivity analysis is a mandatory step.
insights.locator: Table 1, The Nemotron 3 Ultra NVFP4 checkpoint section

insights.claim: Attention linear layers in Nemotron 3 Ultra are kept in BF16 rather than quantized, reflecting their higher sensitivity to precision loss compared to MoE expert layers.
insights.speaker: Seonghee Lee
insights.so_what: Attention layers in transformer architectures are a quantization bottleneck; models with fewer or narrower attention heads may achieve deeper compression.
insights.quote: different layers are quantized to different precision formats, chosen according to each layer's sensitivity to the architecture and its impact on model accuracy
insights.locator: The Nemotron 3 Ultra NVFP4 checkpoint section

insights.claim: W4A16 (4-bit weights, 16-bit activations) on Hopper matches or beats W8A8 performance for Nemotron 3 Ultra specifically because W4A16's smaller memory footprint is the only format that allows Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) to fit alongside the model.
insights.speaker: Seonghee Lee
insights.so_what: MTP is a key inference throughput technique; the ability to fit it alongside the model via W4A16 gives Hopper-based deployments meaningful capability advantages.
insights.quote: We found MTP could only fit alongside W4A16 (4-bit weights, 16-bit activations) so W4A16 matches or beats it across the board.
insights.locator: The Nemotron 3 Ultra NVFP4 checkpoint section

themes.theme: Cross-Architecture Single Checkpoint: Hopper and Blackwell Compatibility

insights.claim: Nemotron 3 Ultra's NVFP4 checkpoint runs on both NVIDIA Hopper and Blackwell from a single checkpoint by converting weight format at runtime: W4A16 on Hopper (no native FP4 tensor cores) and W4A4 on Blackwell.
insights.speaker: Seonghee Lee
insights.so_what: Single-checkpoint cross-architecture compatibility reduces deployment friction and extends the addressable market for NVFP4-optimized models to existing Hopper installed base.
insights.quote: A key innovation of the Nemotron 3 Ultra NVFP4 is that a single checkpoint can run on both NVIDIA Hopper and Blackwell. It achieves this by converting the weight format to match the hardware it runs on.
insights.locator: The Nemotron 3 Ultra NVFP4 checkpoint section

insights.claim: Blackwell architecture has native FP4 tensor cores enabling W4A4 inference, while Hopper lacks this and must fall back to W4A16, creating a fundamental inference throughput differentiation between GPU generations.
insights.speaker: Seonghee Lee
insights.so_what: Enterprises running inference-heavy workloads have a concrete hardware upgrade incentive to migrate from Hopper to Blackwell to unlock native W4A4 FP4 throughput.
insights.quote: On Hopper, which lacks native FP4 tensor cores, the serving framework automatically switches to W4A16. On Blackwell, it uses native W4A4.
insights.locator: The Nemotron 3 Ultra NVFP4 checkpoint section

themes.theme: AutoQuantize and BPE Sweep: Systematic Accuracy-Compression Optimization

insights.claim: NVIDIA Model Optimizer's AutoQuantize (mtq.auto_quantize) automates per-layer precision assignment by scoring layer sensitivity and searching for format combinations that meet a target bit budget, making exhaustive manual search unnecessary.
insights.speaker: Seonghee Lee
insights.so_what: AutoQuantize lowers the barrier to production-quality quantization, potentially commoditizing a process that previously required expensive expert iteration.
insights.quote: Instead of a fixed config, you give it a target bit budget (for example auto_quantize_bits=4.8) and a list of candidate formats... It then scores each layer's sensitivity and searches for the per-layer format assignment that meets the budget at the best accuracy

insights.claim: For Nemotron 3 Ultra, 5.03 effective bits-per-element is the accuracy sweet spot: moving from 4.85 to 5.03 BPE improved the AA-LCR long-context benchmark by 2.4 points, with performance flattening beyond 5.03 BPE.
insights.speaker: Seonghee Lee
insights.so_what: The 5.03 BPE data point provides a concrete quantization target for practitioners working with similarly architected MoE models.
insights.quote: going from 4.85 to 5.03 improved the benchmark by 2.4 points, and benchmark performance then flattened again beyond 5.03. This makes 5.03 BPE the sweet spot.

insights.claim: MSE-based scaling reduced per-tensor weight reconstruction error by 27.1% over four-over-six scaling in Nemotron 3 Ultra experiments, yet produced no consistent improvement on downstream benchmarks, demonstrating that lower reconstruction MSE does not reliably predict better model accuracy.
insights.speaker: Seonghee Lee
insights.so_what: Calibration quality metrics like weight MSE are poor proxies for downstream task performance; end-to-end benchmark evaluation is required to validate quantization choices.
insights.quote: lower MSE does not always translate to better model accuracy. MSE calibration reduced per-tensor weight error by 27.1% over four-over-six scaling in our Nemotron 3 Ultra experiments, yet produced no consistent improvement on downstream benchmarks.

themes.theme: Distributed Quantization Pipeline: Speed and Scalability

insights.claim: Using Megatron-LM with expert parallelism (EP=16, DP=16) across 16 B300 GPUs reduces Nemotron 3 Ultra quantization time from ~85 minutes to ~9 minutes for load and calibration, and total pipeline from 120 to 45 minutes versus the Hugging Face Transformers path.
insights.speaker: Seonghee Lee
insights.so_what: Distributed quantization is essential for 500B+ parameter models; 9.4x faster calibration time reduces the iteration cost of quantization R&D at frontier scale.
insights.quote: This reduces load and calibration from ~85 minutes to ~9.
insights.locator: Table 4, How we quantized Nemotron 3 Ultra to NVFP4 section

insights.claim: The Megatron-LM quantization path for Nemotron 3 Ultra requires model format conversion from Hugging Face to Megatron-LM as an initial step before distributed quantization can proceed.
insights.speaker: Seonghee Lee
insights.so_what: Checkpoint format conversion adds pipeline complexity that developers must account for in production workflows.
insights.quote: the pretrained checkpoint is first converted to Megatron-LM format and then quantized with a single call to quantize.sh
insights.locator: How we quantized Nemotron 3 Ultra to NVFP4 section

insights.claim: The one-click Model Optimizer launcher automates the entire Nemotron 3 Ultra PTQ and export workflow via a single command, requiring only access to a Slurm cluster with sufficient GPU resources (validated on four nodes with four Blackwell GPUs each).
insights.speaker: Seonghee Lee
insights.so_what: Automated end-to-end quantization pipelines lower the operator skill bar for frontier-scale model optimization, enabling more enterprises to deploy NVFP4 models.
insights.quote: the workflow can be launched via the Nemotron 3 Ultra YAML recipe with a single command from a local machine

themes.theme: Prior Model Lessons: Nemotron 3 Super Quantization Iterations

insights.claim: Naive absmax NVFP4 PTQ on Nemotron 3 Super left an accuracy gap that required evaluating alternatives including MSE-based weight scaling and GPTQ, showing that default calibration strategies are insufficient for production-quality FP4 models.
insights.speaker: Seonghee Lee
insights.so_what: First-generation FP4 quantization attempts will likely show accuracy gaps; developers should budget for calibration iteration cycles rather than expecting plug-and-play quality.
insights.quote: naive absmax NVFP4 PTQ left an accuracy gap, so the team evaluated a range of alternative calibration strategies

insights.claim: The optimal NVFP4 configuration for Nemotron 3 Super combined MSE-based block scaling for weights with a per-tensor FP8 sweep and dynamic max-based scaling for activations, while Nemotron 3 Ultra required a different approach (four-over-six), showing that optimal quantization recipes are model-specific.
insights.speaker: Seonghee Lee
insights.so_what: There is no universal FP4 quantization recipe; each new model architecture requires fresh calibration experiments, sustaining demand for quantization tooling and expertise.
insights.quote: Combining MSE weights with the FP8 activation sweep gave the best accuracy-to-size tradeoff of everything we tried, and it became our optimal NVFP4 configuration for Super.

insights.claim: Max (absmax) scaling is highly vulnerable to outlier weights: a single outlier can set the scale such that all other weights in a block are flushed to zero, destroying information and degrading model accuracy.
insights.speaker: Seonghee Lee
insights.so_what: Outlier weight distributions in large models make naive quantization unreliable; models with heavier-tailed weight distributions will suffer more from absmax calibration.
insights.quote: with the presence of a single large weight outlier, the max scaling compresses every other value in the block into a narrow range, which can end up flushing these values to zero.

themes.theme: NVIDIA Model Optimizer: Customizability and Open-Source Ecosystem

insights.claim: NVIDIA Model Optimizer supports customizable YAML quantization recipes that can be applied to any Hugging Face checkpoint, not just NVIDIA's own models, making the toolchain broadly applicable across the open-source model ecosystem.
insights.speaker: Seonghee Lee
insights.so_what: NVIDIA's quantization toolchain becoming an ecosystem standard for HF models extends its software influence beyond its own model portfolio and deepens developer lock-in to CUDA/Blackwell stack.
insights.quote: While we walked through this on Nemotron 3 Ultra, the same pipeline works with any Hugging Face model checkpoint.

insights.claim: NVIDIA Model Optimizer's built-in NVFP4 presets include selective configurations (NVFP4_MLP_ONLY_CFG, NVFP4_EXPERTS_ONLY_CFG, NVFP4_OMLP_ONLY_CFG) that restrict FP4 to specific layer types while keeping sensitive attention projections in higher precision.
insights.speaker: Seonghee Lee
insights.so_what: Granular preset configs lower the iteration cost for developers optimizing accuracy-compression tradeoffs on new model architectures.
insights.quote: The built-in NVFP4 configs range from NVFP4_DEFAULT_CFG, which quantizes broadly, to more selective presets like NVFP4_MLP_ONLY_CFG, NVFP4_EXPERTS_ONLY_CFG, and NVFP4_OMLP_ONLY_CFG
